summary: Matched the menu deflicker module by replacing asm/melee/mn/mndeflicker.s with a new C implementation in src/melee/mn/mndeflicker.c, switching the object to Matching in configure.py and obj_files.mk, and updating symbols/prototypes needed by the decompiled code. The PR had no human body or review comments in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.

key_files.reason: Deleted the 498-line handwritten/disassembled asm source for the module.
Added the matched C implementation with mnDeflicker_8024A168, mnDeflicker_8024A2E8, mnDeflicker_8024A344, mnDeflicker_8024A3E8, mnDeflicker_8024A4BC, and mnDeflicker_8024A6C4.
Changed melee/mn/mndeflicker.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Replaced the asm object with the C object in TEXT_O_FILES.
Renamed local fn_8024A2E8/fn_8024A344/fn_8024A3E8 to mnDeflicker_* names, fixed mnDeflicker_803EEFF0 size from 0xB8 to 0xC, and marked mndeflicker sdata2 constants local.
Updated lbArchive_80016AF0 prototype to accept HSD_Archive*, void*, and varargs as used by the matched C.
Changed HSD_GObj_80391070 second parameter type from s32 to int.
Kept HSD_GObj_80391070 declaration consistent with implementation.
Corrected HSD_SisLib_803A6368 and HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC prototypes for mndeflicker call sites.
Reordered warning flags and added -Wno-unused-variable, likely supporting current decompilation patterns with fake stack/permuter locals.

what_changed: Removed asm/melee/mn/mndeflicker.s and added a 284-line src/melee/mn/mndeflicker.c replacement.
Marked melee/mn/mndeflicker.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py.
Implemented deflicker input/update logic around mn_804D6BC8, mn_804A04F0, mnDeflicker_804D6C38, and mnDeflicker_804D6C3C.

Implemented GObj/JObj setup paths using GObj_Create, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_GObjObjet_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and HSD_GObjPLink_80390228.
Implemented archive loading for MenMainConDf_Top_joint, MenMainConDf_Top_animjoint, MenMainConDf_Top_matanim_joint, and MenMainConDf_Top_shapeanim_joint via lbArchive_80016AF0.
Added small local data structs for gobj user data, sislib placeholder data, and ThreeFloats animation ranges.
Updated symbols.txt so several previously anonymous functions became mnDeflicker_8024A2E8, mnDeflicker_8024A344, and mnDeflicker_8024A3E8.
Adjusted sdata2 symbol scope for mndeflicker float/double constants to local and corrected mnDeflicker_803EEFF0 object size to 0xC.
Adjusted baselib and lbarchive header prototypes to match real call signatures exposed by this decompilation.
smart_moves: Kept address-based mnDeflicker_* names for unknown functions while still replacing fn_* anonymous symbol names, improving searchability without overclaiming semantic names.
Used explicit placeholder structs with offset-style fields such as x0, x2, x4, x8, x10, and x11 where the layout mattered but semantic names were not yet known.
Added a targeted ThreeFloats struct for the repeated animation parameter triples, matching the corrected 0xC data size in symbols.txt.
Used a local inline helper/macro around HSD_GObjGetUserData for user-data access, isolating a matching-sensitive access pattern.
Preserved matching-sensitive temporary variables and comments such as ty_permuter, fake stack arrays, and inline argument notes rather than hiding uncertainty behind premature cleanup.
Added force_float_order_* helper functions and a TODO noting the float-order hack, making the constant-order workaround visible for future cleanup.
Updated shared prototypes in headers rather than relying only on local externs when the matched C revealed better function signatures.
decomp_lessons: When converting an asm module to C, remember to update both configure.py Matching/NonMatching status and obj_files.mk object selection; otherwise the new C may not replace the asm object.
Symbol map maintenance is part of matching: matching a file can reveal local functions, local sdata2 constants, and incorrect object sizes.
Offset-named fields are acceptable when they document known layout while avoiding invented semantics.
Small prototype fixes in shared headers can be required to let a matched translation unit type-check cleanly, especially for varargs archive loaders and baselib helpers.
For float-heavy files, literal ordering can affect rodata/sdata2 layout; document hacks like force_float_order_* so future work can replace them with a cleaner ordering mechanism.
Fake stack arrays and temporary variables may be necessary to reproduce codegen in early matches; compile warning policy may need to tolerate unused variables during this phase.
GObj process flags and user-data ownership/free callbacks are matching-sensitive; the PR preserves GObj_InitUserData with HSD_Free and direct proc->flags_3 assignment.
naming_conventions: Matched functions use the module prefix plus address format: mnDeflicker_8024A168, mnDeflicker_8024A2E8, mnDeflicker_8024A344, mnDeflicker_8024A3E8, mnDeflicker_8024A4BC, mnDeflicker_8024A6C4.
Previously anonymous local functions in symbols.txt were renamed from fn_8024A2E8/fn_8024A344/fn_8024A3E8 to mnDeflicker_* address names.
Unknown struct members keep offset-like names such as x0, x1, x2, x4, x8, x10, x11.
Placeholder uncertainty is explicit in names/comments such as struct sislib_idk, idk, ty_permuter, fake1, fake2, inline_test, and force_float_order_*.
Data symbols retain address-based names such as mnDeflicker_803EEFD8, mnDeflicker_803EEFE4, mnDeflicker_803EEFF0, mnDeflicker_804D6C38, and mnDeflicker_804D6C3C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the asm source only after adding the C source and changing the build to compile the C object.
Used force_float_order_0 through force_float_order_6 to force float constants such as -9.5F, 9.1F, 17.0F, 364.68332F, 38.38772F, and 0.0521F into the needed order.
Used temporary variables as codegen permuters, including storing HSD_GObj_804D7849 into temp_r29 before HSD_GObjObjet_80390A70.
Used fake stack arrays and unused locals to influence stack layout and instruction generation.
Used explicit casts to f32 for animation requests from integer/u8 values.
Added -Wno-unused-variable to compile_flags.txt, consistent with matching tactics that introduce fake or stack-shaping locals.
Used direct proc->flags_3 assignment after HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 to reproduce GObj process flag behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up force_float_order_* hacks in mndeflicker.c or neighboring mn files.
Search for later semantic renames of mnDeflicker_8024A2E8, mnDeflicker_8024A344, mnDeflicker_8024A3E8, mnDeflicker_8024A4BC, and mnDeflicker_8024A6C4.
Check whether struct sislib_idk or gobj_user_data received a proper shared definition after this PR.
Look for later changes to HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC and HSD_SisLib_803A6368 prototypes to confirm the signatures introduced here.
Search for future removals of -Wno-unused-variable or fake stack locals tied to matching cleanups.
